test.command: BROWNIE_NETWORK=mainnet TYPEDENVS_SHUTUP=1 .venv/bin/python -c "import concurrent.futures.process as cfp; ...; pytest tests/test_constants.py -x -p no:pytest_ethereum -W ignore --timeout 120"
test.notes: 6/6 passed. A UnicodeDecodeError in brownie's teardown phase is pre-existing and unrelated to mission changes.

typecheck.notes: 1132 errors in 83 files. Pre-existing baseline: 69 errors in v2.py before mission, 71 after (2 new errors from new methods following same patterns as existing code). v3.py (28 errors) and uniswap.py (7 errors) unchanged. y/constants.py passes clean. Failures are pre-existing technical debt in this codebase.

lint.notes: 7 files need reformatting. 6 were pre-existing before the mission. tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py is new (added by mission) and needs black formatting.

blockingIssues.description: top_pools() is an async generator wrapped by ASyncGeneratorFunction (via ASyncMeta metaclass), which does NOT accept a `sync` keyword argument. The call `self.top_pools(..., sync=False)` on y/prices/dex/uniswap/v2.py:992 will raise `TypeError: UniswapRouterV2.top_pools() got an unexpected keyword argument 'sync'` at runtime. This breaks the entire multi-pool routing feature for any token that doesn't have a direct stable pool. Same bug exists in two test calls (tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py:56,81). Fix: remove `sync=False` from all top_pools() calls in both files.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Remove `sync=False` from the top_pools() call in y/prices/dex/uniswap/v2.py:992 and from test calls at tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py:56,81. The async generator does not support the sync kwarg; it already returns an async iterator. Also run `black tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py` to fix formatting.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Mainnet ROUTING_TOKENS entry uses runtime weth.address/usdc.address (chain-sensitive). Safe in practice since only ROUTING_TOKENS.get(CHAINID, []) is accessed, but semantically incorrect for non-mainnet chains.

L2 addresses in ROUTING_TOKENS intentionally differ from existing bridged contract objects (native tokens chosen for better liquidity). No documentation of this divergence exists for future maintainers.
UniswapRouterV2.check_liquidity uses mutable default `ignore_pools=[]` (line 1032). Pre-existing antipattern, not introduced by this commit.
The `_ = amount` idiom in V2 check_liquidity (line 915) is unconventional. A comment like `# amount unused in V2` would be clearer.

USDC is included in ROUTING_TOKENS, so 16 wasteful TOKEN→USDC→USDC paths are generated per call (USDC as both intermediary and destination). Fix: skip routing_token == usdc.address alongside existing self-loop check.

Missing integration test for the core feature value: token with USDT V3 pool but no USDC/WETH pool returning valid price. Mission AGENTS.md documents SIX token (0x61C6eBf443AD613c9648762585B3cFD3BA1F3fA8) at block 18000000 as a suitable test case.
Path count grew from 8 to 68 per call on mainnet (8.5× more RPC calls). Acceptable given igather parallelism, but the 16 wasteful USDC→USDC paths exacerbate this.
black formatting fails on tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py. Run: black tests/prices/dex/test_v2_multipool.py
top_pools() on non-mainnet chains (where _supports_factory_helper is True) delegates to deepest_pool_for() which returns only a single pool, effectively disabling multi-pool feature on those chains.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'a_sync: Methods vs Async Generators' section to .factory/library/architecture.md documenting that ASyncGeneratorFunction-wrapped async generators do NOT support the sync kwarg, unlike @a_sync.a_sync-decorated methods. Includes code example showing correct vs incorrect usage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a coding convention: 'When calling async generators on ASyncGenericBase subclasses, do NOT pass sync=True/False — only @a_sync.a_sync-decorated methods support that kwarg. Use `async for item in self.method()` directly.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v2-multipool-candidates feature had a blocking runtime bug (TypeError) from passing sync=False to an async generator (top_pools). The architecture.md library note has been added, but a worker-facing rule in AGENTS.md would prevent recurrence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note about the intentional divergence between ROUTING_TOKENS L2 addresses and the existing bridged contract objects: 'ROUTING_TOKENS uses native token addresses for L2 chains, not the existing bridged usdc/usdt Contract objects, because native tokens have deeper liquidity.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: routing-tokens-constants review flagged this divergence as undocumented, which could confuse future maintainers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In python-lib-worker skill's 'Finding Test Tokens' section, add: 'Check mission AGENTS.md for a pre-documented test tokens table BEFORE searching on-chain.' v3-multihop-expansion worker used MIC (which failed) instead of the SIX token documented in mission AGENTS.md.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both v3-multihop-expansion and v2-multipool-candidates reviewers noted that the mission AGENTS.md 'Useful Test Tokens' section was underutilized.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In python-lib-worker skill, add a note about the async generator sync kwarg limitation as a common pitfall: 'Before calling any method with sync=False, verify it is decorated with @a_sync.a_sync (not a plain async generator). Async generators on ASyncGenericBase get ASyncGeneratorFunction wrapping which does not support sync kwarg.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v2-multipool-candidates blocking bug caused by this exact mistake.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a `test-slow` command that appends --runslow to the test-full command. The --runslow pytest option added in conftest.py is not exposed in services.yaml, making it hard for workers to discover how to run slow V3 integration tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v3-amount-aware-liquidity reviewer noted that @pytest.mark.slow tests are skipped by default and --runslow is undocumented in services.yaml.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents scoped mypy workaround (--follow-imports=skip)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — confirmed in AGENTS.md 'Scoped Validation Commands' section

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents that V3 integration tests should be marked @pytest.mark.slow
